evals.expected_output: A fully scaffolded ACE vault with .obsidian config at ~/obsidian/BrainFrame, added to the vault registry
expectations: Created the directory ~/obsidian/BrainFrame/
Created the +/ folder inside the vault
Created the Atlas/ folder inside the vault
Created Calendar/Daily/ folder inside the vault
Created Calendar/Weekly/ folder inside the vault
Created Calendar/Monthly/ folder inside the vault
Created the Efforts/ folder inside the vault
Created .obsidian/daily-notes.json with folder set to Calendar/Daily
Created .obsidian/core-plugins.json with daily-notes in the enabled list
Registry file ~/.config/obsidian-ace/vaults.json exists and contains an entry with name BrainFrame and path pointing to ~/obsidian/BrainFrame

evals.prompt:
Save this Zustand guide to my Dynasty Nerds vault. It's reference material I'll keep using.

# Zustand State Management

Zustand is a small, fast state management library for React.

## Basic Store
```ts
import { create } from 'zustand'

const useStore = create((set) => ({
  count: 0,
  increment: () => set((state) => ({ count: state.count + 1 })),
}))
```

## With Selectors
Always use selectors to prevent unnecessary re-renders:
```ts
const count = useStore((state) => state.count)
```

## Persist Middleware
```ts
import { persist } from 'zustand/middleware'
const useStore = create(persist((set) => ({ ... }), { name: 'my-store' }))
```
evals.expected_output: File saved in Atlas/ of the Dynasty Nerds vault with Roman numeral folder and alpha file prefix
expectations: File was created inside the Dynasty Nerds vault, not the Personal vault or any other vault
File was placed in Atlas/ (not + or Efforts or Calendar)
The containing folder uses a Roman numeral prefix (e.g. I - , II - , III - )
The file name uses an alpha letter prefix (e.g. A. , B. , C. )
The file contains the Zustand guide content including the code examples
The vault registry was consulted to find the Dynasty Nerds vault path

evals.prompt: Triage the inbox in my Personal vault
evals.expected_output: Read all files in +/, classified each into ACE categories, presented a summary table, and waited for confirmation before moving anything
expectations: Read the contents of the +/ directory in the Personal vault
Read the content of at least 3 files from the +/ inbox
Classified items into ACE categories (Atlas, Calendar, Efforts, or stay/delete)
Presented a structured summary with proposed destinations and reasoning
Did NOT move or delete any files without asking for user confirmation first
Identified any empty files and flagged them for deletion
